solution.text:
S1:  Set of all recursively enumerable languages 
over the alphabet {0, 1} 

   Set of RELs is Countable. 

S2:  Set of all syntactically valid C programs 

   All C, C++, Java, etc. are Countable. 

S3:   Set of all languages over the alphabet {0, 1} 

   Set of all languages = 2* is Uncountable. 

S4:   Set of all non-regular languages over the 
alphabet {0, 1} 

Set of all regular languages is Countable but set of 
all non-regular language is Uncountable.

question.text:
Let A and B be finite alphabets and let # be a symbol 
outside both A and B. Let f be a total function from 
A* to B*. We say f is computable if there exists a 
Turing machine M which given an input x in A*, 
always halts with f(x) on its tape. Let Lf denote the 
language {x # f(x)|x  A*}. Which of the following 
statements is true?

options.text: f is computable if and only if Lf  is recursive.

f is computable if and only if Lf  is recursively 
enumerable.

If f is computable then Lf is recursive, but not 
conversely.

If f is computable then Lf is recursively 
enumerable, but not conversely.

solution.text:
*
*
A
B
f
⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯→


a1 \rightarrow f (a1) = b1 

a2 \rightarrow f (a2) = b2 

a3 \rightarrow f (a3) = b3 

a4 \rightarrow f (a4) = b4 

                    

Lf = {a1# f(a1), a2 #, f(a2)……. } 
• 
Lexicographical order is possible. So, Lf is 
recursive. 
• 
If L is REL then f may/may not be computable. 
• 
If f is computable then Lf is recursively 
enumerable, but not conversely.

question.text:
Let  be a finite non-empty alphabet and let 2* be 
the power set of *. Which one of the following is 
TRUE?

solution.text:
• 
* = Set of all strings = Regular 
Regular is countable. 
• 
2* = Set of all languages  
Set of all languages is uncountable. 
Hence, option (c) is correct.

question.text:
Which of the following pairs have DIFFERENT 
expressive power

options.text:
Deterministic finite automata (DFA) and Non-
deterministic finite automata (NFA)

Deterministic push down automata (DPDA) and 
Non-deterministic push down automata (NPDA)

Deterministic single-tape Turing machine and 
Non-deterministic single - tape Turing machine

Single-tape Turing machine and multi-tape 
Turing machine

solution.text:
• 
DFA ≡ NFA 
• 
DPDA  NPDA 
• 
DTM ≡ NTM whether it is a single tape or 
multi tape.

question.text:
Consider the languages L1, L2 and L3 are given 
below: 

L1 = {0p 1q│p, q  N}, 

L2 = {0p 1q│p, q  N and p = q}  and 

L3 = {0p 1q 0r | p, q, r  N and p = q = r} 

Which of the following statements is NOT TRUE?

options.text:
Push Down Automata (PDA) can be used to 
recognize L1 and L2
All the three languages are context free

Turing machines can be used to recognize all 
the languages

solution.text:
L1 = {0p1q│p, q  N} 0* 1* \Rightarrow Regular 

L2 = {0p1q│p, q  N and p = q}  and \Rightarrow DCFL 

L3 = {0p1q0r |p, q, r  N and p = q = r} \Rightarrow CSL 

Hence, option (c) is correct.

question.text:
Which of the following is true for the language {ap | 
p is a prime number}?

options.text: It is not accepted by a Turning Machine
It is regular but not context-free
It is context-free but not regular

It is neither regular nor context-free, but 
accepted by a Turing machine 

Recursive and RE Language Identification

solution.text:
• 
{ap | p is a prime number} it is a CSL language. 
Every CSL is recursive and RE. 
Hence, option (d) is correct.

question.text: Which of the following statements is/are correct?

options.text:
The intersection of two redular languages is 
regular.

The intersection of two context-free languages 
is context-free.

The intersection of two recursive languages is 
recursive.

The intersection of two recusively enumerable 
languages is recursively enumerable.

solution.text:
(a)  The intersection of two redular languages is 
regular.  


 Reg  Reg \Rightarrow Reg   
True 

(b)  The intersection of two context-free languages 
is context-free.  


 CFL  CFL \Rightarrow CSL   
False 

(c)  The intersection of two recursive languages is 
recursive. 


 Rec  Rec \Rightarrow Rec   
True 

(d) The intersection of two recusively enumerable 
languages is recursively enumerable. 


 REL  REL \Rightarrow REL  
True

L1 = {aP |p is a prime number}  \Rightarrow Not CFL 

L2 = {an bm c2m | n \ge 0, m \ge 0} \Rightarrow DCFL 

L3 = {an bn c2n | n \ge 0} \Rightarrow Not CFL 

L4 = {an bn |n \ge 1} \Rightarrow DCFL

options.text:
Complement of L1 is context-free but not 
regular

solution.text:

L1 = {0p1q0r | p, q, r \ge 0} 
     = 0*1*0* is Regular language 
• 
L2 = {0p1q0r | p, q, r \ge 0 p \neq r} 
     = DCFL language 
• 
L1  L2 = Regular  DCFL = DCFL

question.text: Which of the following statements is / are TRUE?

options.text:
Every subset of a recursively enumerable 
language is recursive.

If a language L and its complement L are both 
recursively enumerable, then L must be 
recursive.

Complement of a context-free language must be 
recursive.

If L1 and L2 are regular, then L1  L2 must be 
deterministic context - free.

solution.text:
(a) Every subset of a recursively enumerable 
language is recursive. FALSE 


Because subset is not closed under any 
language. 


(b) If a language L and its complement L are both 
recursively enumerable, then L must be 
recursive. TRUE 

(c) CFL = CSL 


Every 
CSL 
is 
recursive 
and 
recursive 
enumerable. 

(d) Regular  Regular = Regular 


Every Regular language is DCFL, CFL, 
Recursive and RE.

question.text:
Let <M> denote an encoding of an automaton M. 
Suppose that Σ = {0, 1}. Which of the following 
languages is/are NOT recursive?

options.text: L = {<M> | M is a DFA such that L(M) = ∅}
L = {<M> | M is a DFA such that L(M) = Σ*}
L = {<M> | M is a PDA such that L(M) = ∅}
L = {<M> | M is a PDA such that L(M) = Σ*}

solution.text:
(a) Emptiness for DFA is decidable (Recursive). 

 (b) Totality for DFA is decidable (Recursive). 

 (c) Emptiness for PDA is decidable (Recursive). 

(d) Totality for PDA is undecidable (Not 
Recursive).

question.text: The set of all recursively enumerable languages is

options.text: A subset of the set of all recursive language.

solution.text:
The set of all recursively enumerable language is 
Countable and closed under Intersection but not 
closed under Complementation. 

The set of all RELs is superset of Set of all 
recursive languages.

question.text:
Let L be a language and L be its complement. Which 
one of the following is NOT a viable possibility?

options.text: Neither L nor L is recursively enumerable (r.e.).

One of L and L is r.e. but not recursive; the 
other is not r.e.
Both L and L are r.e. but not recursive.
Both L and L are recursive.

solution.text:
(a) Neither L nor L is recursively enumerable 
(r.e.). 
It is possible that both L and L are not RELs.  
(b) One of L and L is r.e. but not recursive; the 
other is not r.e. 
If L is RE but not REC, then L is always NOT 
RE. 
(c) It is impossible to have both L and L are r.e. 
but not recursive.  
(d) Both L and L are recursive. 
If L is recursive, L must be recursive. 
Hence, option (c) is correct.

question.text:
Let L1 be a recursive language. Let L2 and L3 be 
language that are recursively enumerable but not 
recursive. Which of the following statements is not 
necessarily true?

solution.text:
L1 = Recursive 

L2 = RE but not Recursive 

L3 = RE but not Recursive 
• 
L2 - L1 = L2  
1L 

                     = RE but not Recursive  Recursive 

                     = RE 
• 
L1 - L3 = L1  
L 

                     = L1  NOT REL 

                   = Need not be RE 
• 
RE but not Rec  RE but not Rec = RE. 
• 
RE but not Rec  RE but not Rec = RE.

• 
L1 = {am bm can bn | m, n \ge 0} 
            = CFL 
• 
L2 = {aibjck | i, j, k \ge 0} 
                     =a*b*c* = Regular  
• 
L = L1  L2 

          = am bm c = DCFL 

Hence, option (c)is correct

question.text: Which of the following statements is false?

options.text:
Every NFA can be converted to an equivalent 
DFA.

Every non-deterministic Turing machine can be 
converted to an equivalent deterministic Turing 
machine.

Every regular language is also a context-free 
language.

Every subset of a recursively enumerable set is 
recursive.

solution.text:
Subset is not closed under any language. 

Hence, option (d) is correct.

question.text: If L and L are recursively enumerable then L is

solution.text:
L and L are recursively enumerable if and only if L 
is recursive. 

Hence, option (d) is correct.
